title: To-do (May 2022) — OSIG workbook, flip phone, Bob Dylan, master plan
html:
To-do (May 2022) — OSIG workbook, flip phone, Bob Dylan, master plan

Source: 2022-05-06 Apple Note. A 21-line spring-term todo list — coursework, OSIG mechanics, a Bob Dylan ticket, a flip-phone purchase, and an explicit "start chilling on master plan" line.

TL;DR

  • OSIG mechanics: "OSIG workbook" + "Project Pitch" — Yahir was building the workbook + pitch for OSIG (Oregon State Investment Group) as a member working toward exec.
  • Coursework: MTH quiz, "4 year academic plan for advising appointment" — the standard sophomore-spring grind plus the explicit four-year-plan deliverable that universities trigger around this point.
  • Career probe: "apply to analytics job position" — a one-line job-app note. Doesn't specify which company; doesn't recur in the corpus.
  • The "master plan" line: "start chilling on master plan." Yahir already calling his life-document a "master plan" by spring 2022 — well before the Austin thesis crystallized it into a real document. The verb "chilling on" is interesting — he's deliberately easing off, not pushing harder. Strategic deceleration of long-range planning while term-time is busy.
  • The flip-phone idea: "Buy a flip phone" — sits alone, no commentary. First appearance in the corpus of Yahir's recurring dumb-phone curiosity, an attention-reform instinct that comes back later.
  • The Bob Dylan ticket appears twice (with the misspelling "bob bylaw tickets" the second time) — Yahir was actively trying to buy a Dylan show ticket in early May 2022.
  • Mom-gift line: "buy mom gift" — likely Mother's Day adjacent (May 8 was Mother's Day 2022). Connects to the broader mom care thread that runs across the vault.
  • JCEF + RCC + Linn-Benton: "call rcc and linn benton about classes" + "send info to jcef ✅" — Yahir managing the JCEF scholarship cohort communication while exploring summer credits at community colleges. The RCC / LBCC dual-track is a recurring credit-stacking move.

Pattern observed

This note is the earliest corpus appearance of "master plan" as a phrase — Yahir naming the document-of-his-life two years before the Austin / Outpace / mom house cluster gave it real content. The flip-phone line is also a small but recurring dumb-phone instinct worth tracking across later notes.

Pure form: org logistics, school logistics, a concert ticket, a personal-tech provocation, and a single life-document framing line — all in one undated stack.

Key snippets

  • "Start chilling on master plan."
  • "Buy a flip phone."
  • "OSIG workbook / Project Pitch."
